ops.text: `ccx vcs ship` failed with a raw non-fast-forward push rejection ("git push: exit status 1 ... HEAD -> main (non-fast-forward)") when a concurrent session advanced origin/main mid-ship, despite AGENTS.md documenting that ship "fetches first and rebases the stack onto the target bookmark" in-call. Either the fetch-then-push window raced (the concurrent push landed between them, with no retry loop) or the rebase path never fired in this plain-git (non-jj-colocated) clone — /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-context has no .jj dir, so the documented jj-based recovery (`jj git fetch` + `jj rebase`) was also unavailable. Recovery was manual: cherry-pick the formed commit onto origin/main in a temp worktree, push from there, `git reset --keep origin/main` to realign without touching another lane's dirty files. Ship could retry the fetch+rebase once on a non-fast-forward rejection, and its git fallback should mirror the jj-mode remote-update handling.
